questions.question_text: Ammonolysis of ethyl chloride followed by reaction of the amine so formed with 1 mole of methyl chloride gives an amine that:

board_style_answer.structured_answer: Option (c): reacts with Benzenesulphonyl chloride to form a product that is insoluble in alkali.

examiner_intent_analysis.why_this_question_is_asked: Tests sequential synthesis (EtCl -> EtNH2 -> EtNHMe) and then applying Hinsberg test logic for Secondary Amines.
examiner_intent_analysis.common_student_mistake: Identifying the product as primary or tertiary amine, leading to wrong Hinsberg result.
examiner_intent_analysis.trap_zone: Secondary amines react with Hinsberg reagent but the product has no acidic H, so it's insoluble in alkali.

options.a: reacts with Hinsberg reagent to form a product soluble in alkali
options.b: on reaction with Nitrous acid, produced nitrogen gas
options.c: reacts with Benzenesulphonyl chloride to form a product that is insoluble in alkali
options.d: does not react with Hinsberg reagent

questions.question_text:
The correct increasing order of basic strength for the following compounds is:
(I) Aniline, (II) p-Nitroaniline, (III) p-Toluidine

board_style_answer.structured_answer: Option (a): II < I < III

examiner_intent_analysis.why_this_question_is_asked: Effect of substituents: EWG (-NOâ‚‚) decreases basicity; EDG (-CHâ‚ƒ) increases basicity.
examiner_intent_analysis.common_student_mistake: Confusing the direction of the inequality signs.
examiner_intent_analysis.trap_zone: p-Nitroaniline is the weakest base due to strong -R effect.

questions.question_text: Which of the following compounds will give a positive Carbylamine test?

board_style_answer.structured_answer: Option (b): Aniline

examiner_intent_analysis.why_this_question_is_asked: Test specific for Primary Amines (Aliphatic & Aromatic).
examiner_intent_analysis.common_student_mistake: Selecting secondary or tertiary amines.
examiner_intent_analysis.trap_zone: N-methylaniline (Secondary) does not give this test.

questions.question_text: Gabriel Phthalimide synthesis is used for the preparation of:

board_style_answer.structured_answer: Option (a): Primary aliphatic amines

examiner_intent_analysis.why_this_question_is_asked: It cannot prepare aromatic amines (aryl halides don't undergo nucleophilic substitution with phthalimide anion) or 2آ°/3آ° amines.
examiner_intent_analysis.common_student_mistake: Thinking it prepares Aniline.
examiner_intent_analysis.trap_zone: Only 1آ° Aliphatic.

questions.question_text: The gas evolved when methylamine reacts with nitrous acid is:

board_style_answer.structured_answer: Option (d): Nitrogen

examiner_intent_analysis.why_this_question_is_asked: Aliphatic primary amines react with HNOâ‚‚ to form alcohols and liberate Nâ‚‚ gas quantitatively.
examiner_intent_analysis.common_student_mistake: Predicting ammonia or NOâ‚‚.
examiner_intent_analysis.trap_zone: Aromatic amines form Diazonium salts at low temp; Aliphatic amines decompose to give Nâ‚‚.

questions.question_text:
Assertion (A): Aniline does not undergo Friedel-Crafts reaction.
Reason (R): The -NHâ‚‚ group of aniline reacts with AlClâ‚ƒ (Lewis acid) to form a salt.

board_style_answer.structured_answer: Option (a): Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.

examiner_intent_analysis.why_this_question_is_asked: Standard exception in electrophilic substitution.
examiner_intent_analysis.common_student_mistake: Thinking -NHâ‚‚ is activating so it should react faster.
examiner_intent_analysis.trap_zone: Formation of salt deactivates the ring (Positive charge on N).

options.a: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.b: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.c: A is true but R is false
options.d: A is false but R is true

questions.question_text:
Arrange the following in decreasing order of pKb values:
Câ‚‚Hâ‚…NHâ‚‚, Câ‚†Hâ‚…NHCHâ‚ƒ, (Câ‚‚Hâ‚…)â‚‚NH, Câ‚†Hâ‚…NHâ‚‚

board_style_answer.structured_answer:
1. Logic: Higher pKb = Weaker Base.
2. Order of Basicity: (Câ‚‚Hâ‚…)â‚‚NH > Câ‚‚Hâ‚…NHâ‚‚ > Câ‚†Hâ‚…NHCHâ‚ƒ > Câ‚†Hâ‚…NHâ‚‚.
   (Dialkyl > Monoalkyl > N-alkyl aniline > Aniline).
3. Order of pKb (Reverse of basicity): Câ‚†Hâ‚…NHâ‚‚ > Câ‚†Hâ‚…NHCHâ‚ƒ > Câ‚‚Hâ‚…NHâ‚‚ > (Câ‚‚Hâ‚…)â‚‚NH.
board_style_answer.stepwise_marking_scheme_breakdown:
1 mark for correct identification of strongest/weakest.
1 mark for correct full order.

examiner_intent_analysis.why_this_question_is_asked: Tests understanding of pKb (inverse to Kb) and structural effects (Alkyl vs Aryl).
examiner_intent_analysis.common_student_mistake: Arranging in order of Basicity instead of pKb.
examiner_intent_analysis.trap_zone: Câ‚†Hâ‚…NHCHâ‚ƒ is stronger base than Aniline but weaker than aliphatic.

questions.question_text:
Give the chemical tests to distinguish between:
(i) Methylamine and Dimethylamine
(ii) Aniline and N-Methylaniline

board_style_answer.structured_answer:
(i) Carbylamine Test:
   - Methylamine (1آ°): Gives foul smelling isocyanide on heating with CHClâ‚ƒ/KOH.
   - Dimethylamine (2آ°): No reaction.
(ii) Azo Dye Test (or Carbylamine):
   - Aniline (1آ° aromatic): Gives orange dye with Benzene Diazonium Chloride (or gives foul smell in Carbylamine test).
   - N-Methylaniline (2آ°): No dye/No reaction in Carbylamine.

examiner_intent_analysis.why_this_question_is_asked: Practical organic chemistry application.
examiner_intent_analysis.common_student_mistake: Using Hinsberg test for (ii) is valid but complex to describe observation (insoluble vs soluble). Carbylamine is more direct.
examiner_intent_analysis.trap_zone: N-methylaniline is aromatic but secondary.

questions.question_text:
Write the structures of A, B and C in the following reaction sequence:
Câ‚†Hâ‚…NOâ‚‚ --(Fe/HCl)--> A --(NaNOâ‚‚/HCl, 273K)--> B --(Câ‚†Hâ‚…OH)--> C

board_style_answer.structured_answer:
1. A = Câ‚†Hâ‚…NHâ‚‚ (Aniline).
2. B = Câ‚†Hâ‚…Nâ‚‚âپ؛Clâپ» (Benzenediazonium chloride).
3. C = p-Hydroxyazobenzene (Orange Dye) [Structure: Câ‚†Hâ‚…-N=N-Câ‚†Hâ‚„-OH].

examiner_intent_analysis.why_this_question_is_asked: Sequence: Reduction -> Diazotization -> Coupling.
examiner_intent_analysis.common_student_mistake: Drawing coupling at meta position (Coupling occurs at para to -OH).
examiner_intent_analysis.trap_zone: pH condition for coupling (alkaline for phenol) is implied.

questions.question_text:
Account for the following:
(i) pK_b of aniline is more than that of methylamine.
(ii) Ethylamine is soluble in water whereas aniline is not.
(iii) Methylamine in water reacts with ferric chloride to precipitate hydrated ferric oxide.

board_style_answer.structured_answer:
(i) In aniline, the lone pair on Nitrogen is involved in resonance with the benzene ring, making it less available for protonation. In methylamine, +I effect of methyl group increases electron density on N. Hence Aniline is weaker base (Higher pKb).
(ii) Ethylamine forms H-bonds with water. In aniline, the large hydrophobic phenyl group outweighs the H-bonding effect, reducing solubility.
(iii) Methylamine is a base. In water, it produces OHâپ» ions: CHâ‚ƒNHâ‚‚ + Hâ‚‚O â‡Œ CHâ‚ƒNHâ‚ƒâپ؛ + OHâپ». These OHâپ» ions react with Feآ³âپ؛ to precipitate Feâ‚‚Oâ‚ƒ.xHâ‚‚O.

examiner_intent_analysis.why_this_question_is_asked: Conceptual reasoning.
examiner_intent_analysis.common_student_mistake: In (iii), not realizing it's a simple precipitation reaction due to basicity.
examiner_intent_analysis.trap_zone: pK_b relation (High pK_b = Weak base).

questions.question_text:
How will you convert:
(i) Ethanoic acid to Methanamine
(ii) Hexanenitrile to 1-Aminopentane
(iii) Methanol to Ethanoic acid

board_style_answer.structured_answer:
(i) CHâ‚ƒCOOH --(NHâ‚ƒ, خ”)--> CHâ‚ƒCONHâ‚‚ --(Brâ‚‚/KOH)--> CHâ‚ƒNHâ‚‚ (Hoffmann Bromamide - Step down).
(ii) Câ‚…Hâ‚پâ‚پCN --(Hâ‚ƒOâپ؛)--> Câ‚…Hâ‚پâ‚پCOOH --(NHâ‚ƒ, خ”)--> Câ‚…Hâ‚پâ‚پCONHâ‚‚ --(Brâ‚‚/KOH)--> Câ‚…Hâ‚پâ‚پNHâ‚‚ (1-Aminopentane). (Step down from Nitrile C6 to Amine C5).
(iii) CHâ‚ƒOH --(PClâ‚…)--> CHâ‚ƒCl --(KCN)--> CHâ‚ƒCN --(Hâ‚ƒOâپ؛)--> CHâ‚ƒCOOH (Step up).

examiner_intent_analysis.why_this_question_is_asked: Tests Hofmann Bromamide (Step down) and Cyanide synthesis (Step up).
examiner_intent_analysis.common_student_mistake: Reducing Nitrile directly gives Amine with SAME carbons. Question asks for one less carbon (Hexane to Pentane).
examiner_intent_analysis.trap_zone: Hexanenitrile has 6 Carbons. 1-Aminopentane has 5. Needs Step Down.

questions.question_text: An aromatic compound 'A' with molecular formula Câ‚‡Hâ‚†Oâ‚‚ undergoes a series of reactions as shown below. 'A' on reaction with Ammonia and heating gives 'B'. 'B' on heating with Brâ‚‚ and KOH gives compound 'C' with molecular formula Câ‚†Hâ‚‡N. 'C' reacts with CHClâ‚ƒ and alc. KOH to give a foul smelling compound 'D'. 'C' also reacts with Benzene sulphonyl chloride to give 'E' which is insoluble in alkali. Identify A, B, C, D, E.

board_style_answer.structured_answer:
1. Analysis of C (Câ‚†Hâ‚‡N): Reacts with CHClâ‚ƒ/KOH (Carbylamine) -> Primary Amine. Formula matches Aniline (Câ‚†Hâ‚…NHâ‚‚).
2. B -> C (Aniline) via Brâ‚‚/KOH (Hoffmann Bromamide). So B is Benzamide (Câ‚†Hâ‚…CONHâ‚‚).
3. A -> B (Benzamide) via NHâ‚ƒ/Heat. A is Benzoic Acid (Câ‚†Hâ‚…COOH). Formula Câ‚‡Hâ‚†Oâ‚‚ matches.
4. C (Aniline) + CHClâ‚ƒ/KOH -> D (Phenyl Isocyanide Câ‚†Hâ‚…NC).
5. C (Aniline) + PhSOâ‚‚Cl -> E (N-Phenylbenzenesulphonamide). 'E' contains acidic H on N, so it SHOULD be soluble in alkali. Wait. Question says 'E is insoluble'. Let's check. Primary amine + Hinsberg -> N-alkyl/aryl sulphonamide -> Soluble in alkali. Secondary amine -> Insoluble. 
   *Correction*: Aniline is Primary. Product is Soluble. Did I identify C wrong? 
   If C is Secondary, Carbylamine would be negative. But 'D' is foul smelling, so C IS Primary.
   Maybe the question implies 'E' precipitates out? Or maybe 'C' is N-methylaniline? No, C6H7N is Aniline. N-methylaniline is C7H9N.
   *Standard Board Logic*: Sometimes 'insoluble' is used for the solid precipitate before adding alkali, or a trap. But standard text says: 1آ° Amine -> Soluble. 2آ° Amine -> Insoluble.
   *Re-reading PYQ context*: Sometimes questions have typos. Or maybe 'C' is Benzylamine? C6H5CH2NH2 (C7H9N). Formula doesn't match C6H7N.
   *Decision*: C must be Aniline (C6H7N). E is N-Phenylbenzenesulphonamide. Textually it is soluble in alkali. If question insists 'insoluble', it's a contradiction or I should note "(Technically soluble, but usually precipitates as solid)". 
   *Wait*, let's check N-methylaniline: No Carbylamine. 
   Let's stick to Aniline. A=Benzoic Acid, B=Benzamide, C=Aniline, D=Phenyl Isocyanide, E=N-Phenylbenzenesulphonamide.

examiner_intent_analysis.why_this_question_is_asked: Hoffmann Degradation + Carbylamine loop.
examiner_intent_analysis.common_student_mistake: Identifying 'E' solubility correctly.
examiner_intent_analysis.trap_zone: Hinsberg test solubility rules.

questions.question_text: The major product formed when Aniline is treated with Bromine water is:

board_style_answer.structured_answer: Option (d): 2,4,6-Tribromoaniline

examiner_intent_analysis.why_this_question_is_asked: -NHâ‚‚ is highly activating. Bromine water (polar solvent) leads to poly-substitution.
examiner_intent_analysis.common_student_mistake: Predicting mono-substitution (p-Bromoaniline). That requires protection (Acetylation) or non-polar solvent.
examiner_intent_analysis.trap_zone: Bromine WATER vs Bromine in CSâ‚‚.

questions.question_text:
Give reasons:
(i) Acetylation of aniline reduces its activation effect.
(ii) CHâ‚ƒNHâ‚‚ is more basic than Câ‚†Hâ‚…NHâ‚‚.
(iii) Although -NHâ‚‚ is o/p directing, nitration of aniline gives a significant amount of m-nitroaniline.

board_style_answer.structured_answer:
(i) In acetanilide, the lone pair of nitrogen is involved in resonance with the carbonyl group (-CO-). This decreases the electron density on nitrogen and the benzene ring, reducing activation.
(ii) In methylamine, +I effect of methyl group increases electron density on N. In aniline, resonance (-R) delocalizes the lone pair into the ring, making it less available for protonation.
(iii) Nitration is carried out in strongly acidic medium. Aniline gets protonated to form Anilinium ion (Câ‚†Hâ‚…NHâ‚ƒâپ؛). The -NHâ‚ƒâپ؛ group is electron withdrawing and meta-directing.
board_style_answer.final_answer_boxed: Resonance with CO; Protonation to Anilinium

examiner_intent_analysis.why_this_question_is_asked: The 'Meta product in nitration' is a favorite board question.
examiner_intent_analysis.common_student_mistake: Forgetting the 'Anilinium ion' formation in (iii).
examiner_intent_analysis.trap_zone: Acetanilide resonance involves Oxygen, not just Ring.

questions.question_text: Write the reactions involved in the 'Hinsberg Test' to distinguish between Câ‚‚Hâ‚…NHâ‚‚ and (Câ‚‚Hâ‚…)â‚‚NH.

board_style_answer.structured_answer:
1. Ethylamine (1آ°) + PhSOâ‚‚Cl â†’ N-Ethylbenzenesulphonamide (Soluble in alkali due to acidic H).
   Reaction: PhSOâ‚‚Cl + Câ‚‚Hâ‚…NHâ‚‚ â†’ PhSOâ‚‚NHCâ‚‚Hâ‚… + HCl
2. Diethylamine (2آ°) + PhSOâ‚‚Cl â†’ N,N-Diethylbenzenesulphonamide (Insoluble in alkali, no acidic H).
   Reaction: PhSOâ‚‚Cl + (Câ‚‚Hâ‚…)â‚‚NH â†’ PhSOâ‚‚N(Câ‚‚Hâ‚…)â‚‚ + HCl
board_style_answer.stepwise_marking_scheme_breakdown:
1 mark for Primary amine reaction/observation.
1 mark for Secondary amine reaction/observation.

examiner_intent_analysis.why_this_question_is_asked: Understanding the chemical basis of the test.
examiner_intent_analysis.common_student_mistake: Not showing the structure of the product clearly.
examiner_intent_analysis.trap_zone: Acidity of the N-H hydrogen in the sulfonamide.

questions.question_text:
Read the passage:
Amines are derivatives of ammonia. Their basic character depends on structure and solvation. In gaseous phase, basicity follows inductive effect order. In aqueous phase, steric hindrance and solvation (H-bonding) also play roles. Aryl amines are weaker bases.
(a) Arrange in increasing basicity in gas phase: Câ‚‚Hâ‚…NHâ‚‚, (Câ‚‚Hâ‚…)â‚‚NH, (Câ‚‚Hâ‚…)â‚ƒN, NHâ‚ƒ.
(b) Why is (CHâ‚ƒ)â‚‚NH more basic than (CHâ‚ƒ)â‚ƒN in aqueous solution?
(c) Identify the product formed when Aniline reacts with Acetyl chloride.

board_style_answer.structured_answer:
(a) Gas Phase: NHâ‚ƒ < Câ‚‚Hâ‚…NHâ‚‚ < (Câ‚‚Hâ‚…)â‚‚NH < (Câ‚‚Hâ‚…)â‚ƒN.
   (Order follows +I effect: 3آ° > 2آ° > 1آ° > NHâ‚ƒ).
(b) In aqueous phase, Trimethylamine (3آ°) suffers from steric hindrance to H-bonding solvation of its conjugate acid. Dimethylamine (2آ°) has a better balance of Inductive effect and Solvation stability. Hence 2آ° > 3آ° (for methyl).
(c) Acetanilide (Câ‚†Hâ‚…NHCOCHâ‚ƒ).
board_style_answer.stepwise_marking_scheme_breakdown:
1 mark for (a).
2 marks for (b) (Mention Steric hindrance to Solvation).
1 mark for (c).

examiner_intent_analysis.why_this_question_is_asked: The Methyl vs Ethyl basicity order in aqueous phase is crucial.
examiner_intent_analysis.common_student_mistake: Using the same order for gas and aqueous phases.
examiner_intent_analysis.trap_zone: Ethyl group order (2>3>1) differs from Methyl group order (2>1>3).

questions.question_text: Reaction of aniline with NaNOâ‚‚ + HCl at 0-5آ°C gives:

board_style_answer.structured_answer: Option (c): Benzenediazonium chloride

examiner_intent_analysis.why_this_question_is_asked: Preparation of versatile intermediate.
examiner_intent_analysis.common_student_mistake: Predicting Chlorobenzene (Sandmeyer) directly.
examiner_intent_analysis.trap_zone: Temperature must be low (0-5آ°C), else it decomposes to Phenol.

questions.question_text:
Write the main product of the following reactions:
(i) Câ‚†Hâ‚…Nâ‚‚âپ؛Clâپ» + Hâ‚ƒPOâ‚‚ + Hâ‚‚O â†’
(ii) Câ‚†Hâ‚…Nâ‚‚âپ؛Clâپ» + KI â†’
(iii) Câ‚†Hâ‚…Nâ‚‚âپ؛Clâپ» + CHâ‚ƒCHâ‚‚OH â†’

board_style_answer.structured_answer:
(i) Câ‚†Hâ‚† (Benzene) + Nâ‚‚ + Hâ‚ƒPOâ‚ƒ + HCl
(ii) Câ‚†Hâ‚…I (Iodobenzene) + Nâ‚‚ + KCl
(iii) Câ‚†Hâ‚† (Benzene) + Nâ‚‚ + CHâ‚ƒCHO + HCl
board_style_answer.stepwise_marking_scheme_breakdown: 1 mark for each correct organic product (Benzene/Iodobenzene).

examiner_intent_analysis.why_this_question_is_asked: Reduction to benzene and Iodine substitution.
examiner_intent_analysis.common_student_mistake: In (iii), forming Phenol/Ether instead of Benzene (Ethanol acts as reducing agent).
examiner_intent_analysis.trap_zone: Both H3PO2 and Ethanol reduce Diazonium to Benzene.

questions.question_text: Why cannot aromatic primary amines be prepared by Gabriel Phthalimide synthesis?

board_style_answer.structured_answer:
In Gabriel Phthalimide synthesis, the phthalimide anion acts as a nucleophile and attacks the alkyl halide via Sâ‚™2 mechanism. 
Aryl halides do not undergo nucleophilic substitution easily due to partial double bond character of C-X bond and resonance. Hence, they cannot react with phthalimide anion to form N-arylphthalimide.
board_style_answer.stepwise_marking_scheme_breakdown:
1 mark for 'Aryl halides do not undergo nucleophilic substitution'.
1 mark for 'Resonance/Double bond character' reason.

examiner_intent_analysis.why_this_question_is_asked: Links Amines to Haloarenes concepts.
examiner_intent_analysis.common_student_mistake: Saying 'Steric hindrance' (incorrect, it's electronic).
examiner_intent_analysis.trap_zone: N/A

questions.question_text:
Identify A, B, and C:
CHâ‚ƒCHâ‚‚Cl --(KCN)--> A --(LiAlHâ‚„)--> B --(HNOâ‚‚, 0آ°C)--> C

board_style_answer.structured_answer:
1. A = CHâ‚ƒCHâ‚‚CN (Propanenitrile).
2. B = CHâ‚ƒCHâ‚‚CHâ‚‚NHâ‚‚ (Propan-1-amine) [Reduction of Nitrile].
3. C = CHâ‚ƒCHâ‚‚CHâ‚‚OH (Propan-1-ol) [Primary aliphatic amine with HNOâ‚‚ gives alcohol].

examiner_intent_analysis.why_this_question_is_asked: Sequential transformation.
examiner_intent_analysis.common_student_mistake: In B, writing Ethanamine (reduction keeps carbon count). In C, stopping at Diazonium (unstable for aliphatic).
examiner_intent_analysis.trap_zone: HNO2 with aliphatic amine -> Alcohol + N2.

questions.question_text:
Assertion (A): N-Ethylbenzenesulphonyl amide is soluble in alkali.
Reason (R): Hydrogen attached to nitrogen in sulphonamide is strongly acidic.

board_style_answer.structured_answer: Option (a): Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.

examiner_intent_analysis.why_this_question_is_asked: Explains the observation in Hinsberg test.
examiner_intent_analysis.common_student_mistake: N/A
examiner_intent_analysis.trap_zone: The electron withdrawing Sulphonyl group makes the N-H bond polar/acidic.

options.a: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.b: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.c: A is true but R is false
options.d: A is false but R is true

questions.question_text:
Write the IUPAC names of:
(i) Hâ‚‚N-(CHâ‚‚)â‚†-NHâ‚‚
(ii) Câ‚†Hâ‚…-N(CHâ‚ƒ)â‚‚

board_style_answer.structured_answer:
(i) Hexane-1,6-diamine (Common name: Hexamethylenediamine).
(ii) N,N-Dimethylbenzenamine (or N,N-Dimethylaniline).

examiner_intent_analysis.why_this_question_is_asked: Basic check.
examiner_intent_analysis.common_student_mistake: Missing position numbers.
examiner_intent_analysis.trap_zone: N/A

questions.question_text: The source of nitrogen in Gabriel synthesis of amines is:

board_style_answer.structured_answer: Option (b): Phthalimide

examiner_intent_analysis.why_this_question_is_asked: Source tracking.
examiner_intent_analysis.common_student_mistake: Ammonia (Ammonia is used to prepare Phthalimide, but the immediate source in synthesis is Phthalimide).
examiner_intent_analysis.trap_zone: N/A

questions.question_text:
Complete the following reactions:
(i) Câ‚†Hâ‚…NHâ‚‚ + (CHâ‚ƒCO)â‚‚O â†’
(ii) Câ‚†Hâ‚…Nâ‚‚Cl + HBFâ‚„ â†’ ? --(NaNOâ‚‚, Cu, خ”)--> ?
(iii) Câ‚†Hâ‚…NHâ‚‚ + CHClâ‚ƒ + alc. KOH â†’

board_style_answer.structured_answer:
(i) Câ‚†Hâ‚…NHCOCHâ‚ƒ (Acetanilide) + CHâ‚ƒCOOH.
(ii) Câ‚†Hâ‚…Nâ‚‚âپ؛BFâ‚„âپ» --(NaNOâ‚‚, Cu, خ”)--> Câ‚†Hâ‚…NOâ‚‚ (Nitrobenzene) + Nâ‚‚ + NaBFâ‚„.
(iii) Câ‚†Hâ‚…NC (Phenyl Isocyanide) + 3KCl + 3Hâ‚‚O.
board_style_answer.stepwise_marking_scheme_breakdown: 1 mark each. (ii) is Balz-Schiemann variant/Nitro prep.

examiner_intent_analysis.why_this_question_is_asked: Covering Acetylation, Nitro-group introduction, and Carbylamine.
examiner_intent_analysis.common_student_mistake: In (ii), stopping at Fluorobenzene (Balz-Schiemann) if reagents NaNO2/Cu are ignored. With NaNO2/Cu, it forms Nitrobenzene.
examiner_intent_analysis.trap_zone: HBF4 reaction can lead to Fluoro or Nitro depending on next step.

questions.question_text: Why is aniline acetylated before subjecting it to nitration?

board_style_answer.structured_answer: Direct nitration of aniline yields oxidation products (tary matter) and significant meta-nitroaniline (due to anilinium ion formation). Acetylation protects the -NHâ‚‚ group, moderates its activating effect, and prevents protonation, ensuring para-nitroacetanilide is the major product, which can be hydrolyzed back to p-nitroaniline.
board_style_answer.stepwise_marking_scheme_breakdown:
1 mark for 'Prevents oxidation/tar'.
1 mark for 'Prevents meta product/Controls activation'.

examiner_intent_analysis.why_this_question_is_asked: Practical necessity in synthesis.
examiner_intent_analysis.common_student_mistake: Saying 'to make reaction faster' (Acetylation actually slows it down/moderates it).
examiner_intent_analysis.trap_zone: N/A

most_repeated_concepts: Basicity Order (Aqueous vs Gas phase, Alkyl vs Aryl).
Named Reactions (Hoffmann Bromamide, Gabriel Phthalimide, Sandmeyer).
Electrophilic Substitution of Aniline (Protection, Meta product).
high_probability_2026_focus: Reasoning on solubility and pKb values.
chapter_pattern_analysis.case_based_trend: Focuses on Basicity trends and Solubility logic.
chapter_pattern_analysis.assertion_reason_trend: Reactivity of Aniline (Friedel Crafts failure) and Physical properties.
